intent: 审计 issues.jsonl 中 critical+high bug:去重→验真伪(refuted 记理由)→修复 confirmed→回归收敛, 目标 v2.2 rc2+
goal: issues.jsonl 中全部 critical+high 条目完成 triage(confirmed/refuted/dup-merged/already-fixed 均带证据), 其中 confirmed-real 项全部修复且通过 deterministic 验证, 且修复后全仓 tsc+test 绿(无回归). medium/low carry 到 rc3 backlog.

in_scope: issues.jsonl 中 severity=critical|high 的 77 条(去重后 ~64 簇)
去重: 同簇 member 合并并在 issues.jsonl 标 resolution
验真伪: 每条 grep/tsc/read 取证, refuted 标 already-fixed|non-bug|duplicate
修复 confirmed-real 项 + 每 wave 收口 git commit(rc2 节奏)
回归: 每 wave 末 pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit + test 必绿
out_of_scope: severity=medium|low 的 94 条(carry 到 rc3 backlog, 本轮只标记不修)
新功能/重构超出 bug 修复范围
非 issues.jsonl 来源的新发现(另起 finding 但需 alignment 论证)
constraints: 任何 fix 前先 grep 验证声称(防 reimplemented-noop, 见 feedback-audit-verification)
改 packages/shared schema 必须 rebuild dist
release 前本地必跑 pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit(CI tsc≠local build)
分批 commit: 每 wave 收口即 commit 并回填 git_commits[]
已 GA 包烧号约束: 不动 published 版本号, rc tag 递进

findings.title: Full project-tree walk + read+sha256 of every test file on EVERY recall/plan-context call
findings.expectation_source: Cache the knowledge-test index keyed by a cheap stat-based fingerprint (max mtime of knowledge/ + test files, or a dir-listing hash) and only re-walk when it changes; or decouple computeKnowledgeTestIndex from the read-path buildKnowledgeMeta entirely (the test-annotation index is only needed by doctor, not by recall). At minimum, gate findTestFiles behind the same mtime check used to decide whether agents.meta.json actually drifted.

verify.evidence: knowledge-meta-builder.ts:515-566 (ISS-003): read+sha256-per-test-file gated behind annotationCache (mtime/size); cache hit zero content reads (:543-554). Residual readdir-only walk is documented deliberate-cheap retention.

findings.title: buildKnowledgeMeta re-reads + re-hashes every knowledge file on every read-path call (no cache)
findings.expectation_source: Memoize buildKnowledgeMeta output keyed by a directory-fingerprint (file count + max mtime over the knowledge subdirs), reusing the existing contextCache infrastructure / file_watch invalidation. The revision-drift comparison can short-circuit on the fingerprint without reading file bodies when nothing changed.

verify.evidence: knowledge-meta-builder.ts:197-291 (ISS-004): computeKnowledgeBasedAgentsMeta gates per-file read+sha256 behind computeKnowledgeFileSignature; signature match returns cached meta zero file reads (:237-241).

findings.title: Repeated full-corpus loading of event ledger into memory
findings.expectation_source: Parse the ledger once per doctor run and pass the resulting events array to all dependent inspection functions, or use a stream parser.

verify.evidence: event-ledger.ts: readEventLedger reads whole file but bounded by ISS-025 size rotation (50MB trigger→25MB, :272-286) + 30d retention + 6h tick; parse gated by ISS-005 event_type pushdown (:358). Bounded, not unbounded.

findings.title: God-file: doctor.ts (8697 lines)
findings.expectation_source: Decompose into smaller, domain-specific lint and repair services.

verify.evidence: doctor.ts 8696 lines is a maintainability smell, not a behavioral bug. Splitting it is a refactor — explicitly OUT of scope (boundary: 重构超出 bug 修复范围). Carry rc3 tech-debt.

findings.title: Search input removes keyboard focus indicator
findings.expectation_source: Remove outline-none or add a WCAG-compliant focus outline via focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-accent.

verify.evidence: non-applicable: repo 无任何前端源(packages 仅 cli/server/server-http-experimental/shared; 零 focus-visible/treeitem/brand-accent/.tsx/.css 产品源). 该 a11y finding 引用不存在的 web UI 组件(Registry Tree/Sidebar/Header Search)

findings.title: Lock-Reclaim Race Condition and Lock Theft in withFileLock
findings.expectation_source: Write a unique process/session token (UUID) into the exclusive lock file on creation; in finally, only unlink if file contents match the current holder's token.
findings.actual: atomic-write.ts:127 finally 无条件 unlink(lockPath),不校验持有权;line 109-110 reclaim stat→unlink 间无 token 校验。临界区超 staleMs 时 A 的 finally 删 B 锁 = 双进程同时进临界区。

verify.evidence: atomic-write.ts:127 finally 无条件 unlink(lockPath),不校验持有权;line 109-110 reclaim stat→unlink 间无 token 校验。临界区超 staleMs 时 A 的 finally 删 B 锁 = 双进程同时进临界区。

findings.title: Synchronous git process spawning during array sorting (forensic scan)
findings.expectation_source: Pre-calculate git churn weights for all entry points once into a Map, then look them up O(1) in the comparator.

verify.evidence: readGitChurnWeight no longer exists in doctor.ts; collectEntryPoints (:8266) sorts by path.localeCompare (:8306) — the per-comparison sync git spawn the finding describes was removed. grep: no GitChurn references remain.

findings.title: Missing ARIA tree roles and keyboard focusability in registry tree nodes
findings.expectation_source: Wrap the tree container in role=tree, give each node role=treeitem + tabindex=0, and add keydown handlers for arrows and Enter/Space to manage focus/selection.

verify.evidence: non-applicable: repo 无任何前端源(packages 仅 cli/server/server-http-experimental/shared; 零 focus-visible/treeitem/brand-accent/.tsx/.css 产品源). 该 a11y finding 引用不存在的 web UI 组件(Registry Tree/Sidebar/Header Search)

findings.title: Race condition in event ledger truncation

findings.actual: event-ledger.ts:375/385 truncate + doctor.ts:1622 truncateLedgerToLastNewline 无 withFileLock; append 仅 in-process ledgerQueue → 跨进程 append↔truncate race

verify.evidence: event-ledger.ts:375/385 truncate + doctor.ts:1622 truncateLedgerToLastNewline 无 withFileLock; append 仅 in-process ledgerQueue → 跨进程 append↔truncate race

findings.title: Interactive elements lack semantic tags — not keyboard accessible

findings.title: Personal-layer knowledge (KP-*) can leak into team read-set
findings.expectation_source: Enforce required_stores authorization at the knowledge-graph read layer (recall/query), isolating the personal tree by default.
findings.actual: load-active-meta/recall/plan-context 零 layer 隔离, personal KP-* 可进 team read-set

verify.evidence: load-active-meta/recall/plan-context 零 layer 隔离, personal KP-* 可进 team read-set

findings.title: Synchronous Blocking I/O in Hot Edit-Time Hook Paths
findings.expectation_source: Refactor the file persistence helper (state-store.cjs) and hook script to use async operations (fs.promises) or a decoupled background process so writes do not block the active editor loop.

verify.evidence: knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs (PreToolUse) is now READ-ONLY: no appendFileSync/writeFileSync/mkdirSync/counter writes remain (grep empty over 1636 lines). The edit-counter/events writes moved to the Stop hook which routes through appendLockedLine. Remaining reads are bounded + inherent to the synchronous hook protocol.

findings.title: Global cooldown throttle silences hook nudges across concurrent sessions
findings.expectation_source: Scope cache filenames/keys by session_id (archive-hint-shown-{sessionId}.json, maintenance-hint-last-emit-{sessionId}), mirroring session-hints-{session_id}.json.
findings.actual: fabric-hint.cjs:199/205 SHOWN_CACHE/MAINTENANCE cooldown sidecar 无 sessionId, 并发窗口互相静默 nudge; 旁证 session-hints-{id} 已 session-scoped

verify.evidence: fabric-hint.cjs:199/205 SHOWN_CACHE/MAINTENANCE cooldown sidecar 无 sessionId, 并发窗口互相静默 nudge; 旁证 session-hints-{id} 已 session-scoped

findings.title: Interactive elements lack custom focus styles (nav/toggle/copy)
findings.expectation_source: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-accent focus-visible:outline-none to all sidebar nav links and active buttons.

verify.evidence: non-applicable: repo 无任何前端源(packages 仅 cli/server/server-http-experimental/shared; 零 focus-visible/treeitem/brand-accent/.tsx/.css 产品源). 该 a11y finding 引用不存在的 web UI 组件(Registry Tree/Sidebar/Header Search)

findings.title: Personal-layer (KP-*) metadata leaks into git-committed agents.meta.json
findings.expectation_source: Exclude KP-* / ~/.fabric/knowledge entries from the shared .fabric/agents.meta.json; persist personal metadata machine-locally (.fabric/.cache/ or ~/.fabric/). Exclude personal entries from computeRevision.
findings.actual: meta-builder.ts:84 注释明示 KP-* 含入 git-committed agents.meta.json; line286 computeRevision(nodes) 含 personal

verify.evidence: meta-builder.ts:84 注释明示 KP-* 含入 git-committed agents.meta.json; line286 computeRevision(nodes) 含 personal

findings.title: Dormant cross-store reference lint / personal-leak guard never invoked on write path
findings.expectation_source: Import and execute lintCrossStoreReferences and isPersonalLeakIntoSharedStore inside knowledge write-path services (e.g. extractKnowledge) and block persistence on violation.
findings.actual: cross-store-lint.ts:33/74 定义 lintCrossStoreReferences/isPersonalLeakIntoSharedStore, 排除 def+test 后零调用点 = 死守卫, write path 从不拦 cross-store/personal-leak。

verify.evidence: cross-store-lint.ts:33/74 定义 lintCrossStoreReferences/isPersonalLeakIntoSharedStore, 排除 def+test 后零调用点 = 死守卫, write path 从不拦 cross-store/personal-leak。

findings.title: Path traversal -> arbitrary file write via unsanitized project_id in bindings snapshot
findings.expectation_source: Sanitize projectId to strictly alphanumeric (regex/Zod in fabricConfigSchema) and verify the resolved snapshot path starts with the expected global bindings directory.
findings.actual: bindings.ts:26 bindingsSnapshotPath join(...,`${projectId}_resolved.json`) 无消毒, projectId 含 ../ → line58 writeFileSync 任意路径写。

verify.evidence: bindings.ts:26 bindingsSnapshotPath join(...,`${projectId}_resolved.json`) 无消毒, projectId 含 ../ → line58 writeFileSync 任意路径写。

findings.title: PreToolUse hook appends edit-counter / events.jsonl / silence-counter with bare appendFileSync — no advisory lock (multi-window interleave)
findings.expectation_source: Route all three appends through the appendLockedLine pattern (factor it out of injection-log.cjs into lib/ and share it), or at minimum events.jsonl since it is the load-bearing ledger. The counters could alternatively be made session-scoped (filename suffixed with session_id, like hint-dismiss-{sessionId}.json) so concurrent writers never touch the same file.

verify.evidence: Stop/broad hooks (fabric-hint.cjs:1293/1503/1528, knowledge-hint-broad.cjs:974) append to events.jsonl/metrics.jsonl via appendLockedLine (advisory-lock; comment :55-57 'appendFileSync can interleave a partial write; route through the advisory-lock'). The narrow hook no longer writes at all. Bare-append interleave addressed.

findings.title: Synchronous file system reads in full-corpus scans (iterateCanonicalEntries)
findings.expectation_source: Replace sync FS calls with async variants (fs/promises) or cache the canonical entries map across the doctor run.

verify.evidence: iterateCanonicalEntries callers are ALL in doctor.ts (:2975/3073/5791/5818) — a CLI batch command that runs to completion, not the MCP server recall hot path. Sync I/O in a batch CLI does not block a server event loop. (:5634 comment already notes O(N)-per-inspect awareness.)

findings.title: Insufficient text contrast in light mode
findings.expectation_source: Map higher-contrast colors (700 variants) in the light-mode Tailwind theme or darken weight/palette to reach at least 4.5:1.

verify.evidence: non-applicable: repo 无任何前端源(packages 仅 cli/server/server-http-experimental/shared; 零 focus-visible/treeitem/brand-accent/.tsx/.css 产品源). 该 a11y finding 引用不存在的 web UI 组件(Registry Tree/Sidebar/Header Search)

findings.title: O(N * M) nested array intersection loop on recall path
findings.expectation_source: Convert candidateIds and requestedIds to Sets for O(1) membership, yielding O(N+M).

verify.evidence: effectiveIds.filter + requestedIds.includes is O(N*M) but N,M are candidate/requested-id counts (~dozens at most, bounded by top_k=24). At this scale O(N*M) is negligible; converting to a Set is a micro-opt with no measurable impact. Not a high-severity bug.

findings.title: Full events.jsonl read + Zod-validate of every line on every recall (3x per fab_recall), only to surface rare redirects
findings.expectation_source: Add an early per-line event_type prefilter (cheap substring/JSON key check) before Zod safeParse, or maintain a small in-memory cache of the redirect map invalidated on append. De-duplicate the redundant loadIdRedirectMap calls across planContext/recall/getKnowledgeSections within one recall (compute once, thread through). Replace the 4 chained .filter() with a single pass.

verify.evidence: recall path does NOT read ledger 3x — recall.ts:117 only APPENDS one telemetry trace. The 3x readEventLedger is doctor.ts:4575-4577 cite-rollup (cold path). All reads use event_type pushdown skipping JSON.parse+Zod for non-matching lines (ISS-005, event-ledger.ts:358).

findings.title: BM25/locality score recomputed for every item on every comparator invocation (score-in-sort antipattern)
findings.expectation_source: Precompute each item's score once into a parallel array (or Schwartzian transform: map to {item, score} then sort on the cached score), so the comparator only subtracts two numbers. Eliminates the log n score-recompute factor and the repeated locality nested-loop.

verify.evidence: sortDescriptionItems (plan-context.ts:671-694, ISS-006) already uses decorate-sort: scoreDescriptionItem is computed ONCE per item (:686-689) then sorted on the precomputed numeric score. The score-in-comparator antipattern the finding describes (:703-716) no longer exists; comment explicitly documents the prior fix.

findings.title: KnowledgeIdAllocator.allocate() read-modify-write of agents.meta.json counters is not serialized — concurrent allocation yields duplicate stable_ids
findings.expectation_source: Guard the read-modify-write with a cross-process advisory lock on agents.meta.json (O_EXCL lock-file like injection-log.cjs, or proper-lockfile). Loop-based promotion in review.ts amplifies the window, so the lock should ideally span the whole batch or be re-acquired per allocate.

verify.evidence: knowledge-id-allocator.ts:50-65 (ISS-013): allocate() read→mutate→atomic-write wrapped in withFileLock(${metaPath}.lock); cross+in-process serialized. Test concurrent_allocation proves N parallel allocate() mint distinct ids.

findings.title: Corrupt agents.meta.json silently falls back to empty meta, and the next allocate() atomically overwrites the file — destroys all node entries
findings.expectation_source: On JSON.parse failure do NOT proceed to overwrite. Either throw (let doctor surface + repair), or preserve the corrupt file to a .corrupted sidecar (as event-ledger.ts:307 does for ledgers) before any rewrite, and refuse to allocate until reconciled.

verify.evidence: knowledge-id-allocator.ts:90-115 (ISS-014): corrupt JSON now quarantines to .corrupted.{ts} sidecar and THROWS GenericIOError instead of returning empty meta — original file preserved, no silent overwrite. Test corrupt_meta_preservation covers it.

findings.title: Two divergent `extractBody` implementations with different semantics
findings.expectation_source: Delete the private copy in review.ts and import `extractBody` from knowledge-sections.ts. Reconcile the trim() difference deliberately (pick one contract — likely trim at the API boundary, not in the shared stripper) and add a test pinning the chosen behavior.

verify.evidence: ISS-017 already consolidated to a SINGLE shared extractBody in _shared.ts. review.ts:17 imports it; review.ts:258 extractBodyTrimmed is a thin wrapper = extractBody(content).trim() (explicitly named, comment :256). knowledge-sections.ts:19 re-exports the same shared fn. No divergent same-named implementations remain.

findings.title: Vector retrieval embeds the ENTIRE candidate corpus per request — no persisted embedding index
findings.expectation_source: Persist per-entry embeddings keyed by stable_id + content hash (e.g. in agents.meta.json or a sidecar), embed only the query + any entries whose hash changed since last index build. Embedding the full corpus per request defeats the purpose of a cache and makes vectors unusable past a few hundred entries.

verify.evidence: Vector retrieval is OPT-IN (embed_enabled, default OFF — plan-context.ts:362 guard). When off it is a complete no-op (text-only ranking byte-identical). Embedding the corpus per request is the known cost of the opt-in C2 feature; a persisted embedding index is an rc3 enhancement, not a regression in the default path.

findings.title: FabricError.actionHint infrastructure is entirely dead in the CLI — every CLI error shows only a bare message with no remedy
findings.expectation_source: Either (a) wrap runMain in index.ts with a try/catch that calls renderFabricError(err) + process.exit(1) when hasActionHint(err), or (b) migrate the plain throws to FabricError subclasses AND wire the renderer. Pick one; right now the actionHint contract is enforced at construction but never surfaced.

verify.evidence: renderTopLevelError IS wired into the CLI top-level catch (index.ts:42), and it calls renderFabricError when hasActionHint(err) (error-render.ts:70-75). The actionHint infra is live, not dead — the finding's premise ('entirely dead, never called') is false.

findings.title: fabric install scaffolds per-project ledgers (events.jsonl, metrics.jsonl, cite-rollup.jsonl) into .fabric/ but never writes a .gitignore to exclude them
findings.expectation_source: On install, append `.fabric/events.jsonl`, `.fabric/metrics.jsonl`, `.fabric/cite-rollup.jsonl`, `.fabric/events.archive/`, `.fabric/.cache/` to the project's .gitignore (idempotently), or write `.fabric/.gitignore` covering the volatile ledgers, mirroring the STORE_GITIGNORE pattern already used for stores.

verify.evidence: install.ts:597 writeDefaultGitignore writes .fabric/.gitignore covering events.jsonl/metrics.jsonl/cite-rollup.jsonl/injections.jsonl/.cache//*.lock/*.corrupted.* and is CALLED at install.ts:942. Telemetry is gitignored; comment :579-582 documents exactly this fix.

findings.title: Race condition during agents.meta.json counter repairs
findings.expectation_source: Apply optimistic concurrency control (check revision/hash before writing) or a cross-process file lock during meta repairs.

verify.evidence: doctor.ts fixCounterDesync(:8135) + applyIndexDriftFix(:2334) did unlocked read→atomicWriteJson of counters → same clobber class as F39 (sibling write sites). FIXED: both wrapped in withFileLock(${metaPath}.lock).

findings.title: Non-atomic multi-step meta updates
findings.expectation_source: Merge node reconciliation and counter desync repairs into a single read-modify-write atomic transaction on agents.meta.json.

verify.evidence: doctor --fix reconcileKnowledge(writeKnowledgeMeta) + fixCounterDesync were two independent unlocked atomic writes. Each meta write now holds ${metaPath}.lock; combined with the deliberate before/after fixCounterDesync ordering (doctor.ts:1540-1618) the counter-clobber window is closed. Cross-step intermediate visibility is self-healing via the post-reconcile fixCounterDesync (acceptable; full doctor-run lock would block live MCP allocate for seconds).

findings.title: Silent Event Ledger append failure (no visibility to human or AI)
findings.expectation_source: Do not silently swallow; capture non-fatal ledger append failures as a warning / failed-mutation entry in the returned DoctorFixReport/DoctorApplyLintReport so callers see the desync.

verify.evidence: All ledger appends are best-effort by design: emitEventBestEffort (extract-knowledge.ts:907-916) + recall.ts:127 swallow with comment 'Event emission is observability-only — pending file write is source of truth'. Ledger is non-authoritative; source-of-truth writes propagate errors separately.

findings.title: Sensitive data / PII written to disk without fine-grained redaction
findings.expectation_source: Introduce content-based PII/secrets scanning + redaction before knowledge is persisted, and extend redactSecretsInEvent to cover dynamic payload content.

verify.evidence: event-ledger redactSecretsInEvent by FIELD NAME is deliberate (ISS-043, event-ledger.ts:202-214): redacting all fields would mangle structured ids/paths/hashes with false-positive secret matches. Technical secrets ARE redacted on free-text fields + hasSecrets hard-block on KB write. 'deep-clean all payload' conflicts with the documented design. PII coverage is the F48 feature gap, not a regression.

findings.title: Unbounded memory growth in readEventLedger / rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded
findings.expectation_source: Migrate readEventLedger and rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded to readline.createInterface + fs.createReadStream for O(1)-memory linear parsing.

verify.evidence: readEventLedger/rotateEventLedger memory bounded by ISS-025 size rotation (50MB→25MB) + retention + 6h tick (event-ledger.ts:272-286,557-564). Not unbounded.

findings.title: Frontmatter YAML Injection in Modify/Approve/Reject Paths
findings.expectation_source: Use quoteRelevancePath / quoteIfNeeded on each element of tags and relevance_paths before joining.
findings.actual: 与 F36 同根: modify/approve/reject 路径经 quoteIfNeeded 写 frontmatter, 反斜杠未转义

verify.evidence: 与 F36 同根: modify/approve/reject 路径经 quoteIfNeeded 写 frontmatter, 反斜杠未转义

findings.title: Frontmatter Escape via Backslash in quoteIfNeeded (review.ts)
findings.expectation_source: Escape backslashes first (replace(/\\/g,'\\\\')) before escaping double-quotes, matching quoteRelevancePath in extract-knowledge.ts.
findings.actual: review.ts:1449 quoteIfNeeded 转义 " 却不先转义 \, 末尾反斜杠值产出 "…\" 吞掉闭合引号

verify.evidence: review.ts:1449 quoteIfNeeded 转义 " 却不先转义 \, 末尾反斜杠值产出 "…\" 吞掉闭合引号

findings.title: Path Traversal in reverse-unarchive primitive
findings.expectation_source: Add path confinement (like resolveSandboxedPath in review.ts) to guarantee the resolved path stays strictly within .fabric/.archive/.
findings.actual: unarchive-knowledge.ts archivePathRel 无 containment, .fabric/.archive/../../x + targetLayer 越界读+写爬升

verify.evidence: unarchive-knowledge.ts archivePathRel 无 containment, .fabric/.archive/../../x + targetLayer 越界读+写爬升

findings.title: Synchronous Repository Walks and File Reads in Doctor Checks
findings.expectation_source: Refactor deep directory inspections and reads in doctor.ts to async readdir/stat/readFile and parallelize with Promise.all().

verify.evidence: Doctor checks run as a CLI batch command (fabric doctor), not on the MCP server event loop. Synchronous readdirSync/readFileSync/statSync in a to-completion batch tool is acceptable; it does not freeze a long-lived server. Same class as F19.

findings.title: Counter overwrite: writeKnowledgeMeta rebuilds without the allocator lock
findings.expectation_source: Acquire withFileLock on ${metaPath}.lock in writeKnowledgeMeta before reading/computing/writing the metadata, serializing rebuilds against allocator increments.

verify.evidence: writeKnowledgeMeta (knowledge-meta-builder.ts:139) did read-rebuild-write of agents.meta.json preserving counters from a possibly-stale snapshot WITHOUT the allocator's ${metaPath}.lock → could clobber a concurrent allocate()'s counter bump → re-mint duplicate stable_id. Reachable under user's multi-window concurrent-dev. FIXED: wrapped in withFileLock.

findings.title: Cross-process event loss in ledger rotation and drops
findings.expectation_source: Wrap the critical sections of rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded and dropEventsFromLedger in withFileLock on events.jsonl.lock for cross-process mutual exclusion.

verify.evidence: F9/ISS-002: append/rotate/drop/truncate all wrapped in cross-process withFileLock(events.jsonl.lock) (event-ledger.ts:36-40 exclusiveLedgerWrite, :260 append, :392 truncate, :496 rotate, :646 drop). Cross-process mutual exclusion present.

findings.title: Torn writes / page-cache corruption from omission of fsync
findings.expectation_source: Enable fsync by default for critical metadata/config writes (agents.meta.json, settings.json) in KnowledgeIdAllocator and doctor fixes, forcing fd.datasync before rename.

verify.evidence: atomicWriteText uses tmp+rename (atomic, prevents torn writes regardless of fsync — atomic-write.ts:37-39) for ledger rewrites; append crashes handled by partial-tail tolerance (truncateLedgerToLastNewline, event-ledger.ts:387-420); shutdown durability via flushAndSyncEventLedger fsync (:781-790).

findings.title: Boilerplate Copy-Paste Installation Functions for Skills
findings.expectation_source: Use a single data-driven helper installFabricSkill(skillKey, templateRel, options) with skills represented as config objects in a metadata array.

verify.evidence: 7 per-skill install boilerplate functions are duplication (maintainability), not a behavioral bug. Consolidating into a table-driven loop is a refactor — OUT of scope. Carry rc3.

findings.title: Duplicated Process Liveness and Lock Reading Logic across Packages
findings.expectation_source: Consolidate these OS/process utilities in the shared package (e.g. @fenglimg/fabric-shared/node lock-utils.ts) and import in both packages.

verify.evidence: isAlive/readLockState duplicated between server legacy-serve-lock-probe.ts and quarantine server-http-experimental serve-lock.ts. Duplication is a maintainability/divergence-risk smell, not a current bug (logic is identical). Refactor — OUT of scope; one side is workspace-excluded quarantine anyway.

findings.title: Standard Error throws bypass the actionHint error renderer
findings.expectation_source: Throw a FabricError class (or structural FabricErrorShape match) carrying actionHint instead of generic Error.

verify.evidence: renderTopLevelError correctly handles BOTH FabricError (with actionHint arrows) and plain Error (generic message). Plain Errors legitimately have no actionHint. The errors ARE displayed; converting specific install throws to FabricError-with-hint is a UX enhancement (richer remedy text), not a correctness bug.

findings.title: Swallowed cache write failures silently break hooks and nudge logic
findings.expectation_source: Have fabric doctor explicitly check write permissions/health of .fabric/.cache/ and log a diagnostic warning/error if the hook cache cannot be updated.

verify.evidence: writeJsonState/writeTextState swallowing FS errors is deliberate hook resilience — a hook must NEVER crash the host editor process over a best-effort cache write (read-only FS, perms). The 'no diagnostic visibility' gap is an enhancement (a doctor cache-health check), not a regression; the swallow itself is correct.

findings.title: CLI glyphs lack accessible text fallback in color-enabled sessions
findings.expectation_source: Prepend/append textual labels in all color states (e.g. '[ok] ✓', '[warn] !', '[error] x') for a robust visual + spoken fallback.

verify.evidence: Color-mode status glyphs (✓/!/x) are the established UX; NO_COLOR mode already provides [ok]/[warn]/[error] text. Adding text labels alongside glyphs in color mode is an accessibility ENHANCEMENT (rc3 a11y track), not a bug — no incorrect behavior or data loss; the information is present via color+shape.

findings.title: Store diagnostics severity check masks errors as info
findings.expectation_source: Add explicit branches: severity==='error' -> symbol.error, 'warn' -> symbol.warn, else [info].

verify.evidence: StoreDiagnostic.severity is typed ONLY 'warn' | 'info' (doctor-checks.ts:24) — TS forbids any 'error'/'critical' value. The doctor.ts:625 ternary (warn→[warn], else→[info]) is correct for the actual domain; there is NO error severity that could be masked. Finding's premise (critical errors rendered as info) is impossible given the type.

findings.title: No PII scanning/redaction before capturing into KB or event ledger
findings.expectation_source: Add dedicated PII regex rules (emails, IPs, phone numbers) into redactSecrets/scanForSecrets and apply across all telemetry logging and write-paths.

verify.evidence: PII regex rules (emails/IPs/phones) are a MISSING FEATURE — secret-scan.ts never claimed PII coverage, only technical-secret detection (AWS/OpenAI/Slack/GitHub/password). events.jsonl + KB are gitignored per-dev local telemetry (low exposure). Adding PII redaction is an rc3 enhancement, not a bug fix; out of rc2 bug-audit scope.

findings.title: Audit gap: in-place knowledge edits (modifyEntry) emit no ledger event
findings.expectation_source: Emit a knowledge_modified / knowledge_metadata_updated event in the modifyEntry in-place flow capturing stable_id/path, patch changes, original values, and session metadata.

verify.evidence: The event ledger is observability-only (see F32) — the KB frontmatter is the source of truth for maturity/tags/etc. modifyEntry's in-place edit persists to frontmatter (authoritative); a missing knowledge_modified telemetry event is an audit-completeness enhancement, not a correctness bug. No gate/replay depends on it for correctness.

findings.title: metrics.jsonl grows unbounded with NO rotation mechanism
findings.expectation_source: Add size-based and age-based rotation for metrics.jsonl like events.jsonl (archive old rows to a compressed sidecar, keep only active rows).

verify.evidence: metrics.jsonl is a FOLDED counter-row ledger (rc.37 Wave B): one row per flush/drain interval, not per-event (metrics.ts:87-109) — low growth rate. doctor G7/G9/G10 surface size/staleness warnings (events-jsonl-gates.ts). Adding rotation is rc3 enhancement, not critical bug.

findings.title: Full-ledger in-memory scans for events and metrics files scale poorly
findings.expectation_source: Implement index/tail-based streaming scans (read only last N lines or since a timestamp) or a pre-aggregated in-memory index, avoiding full-text parse per call.

verify.evidence: events.jsonl scans bounded by rotation+pushdown (see F3); metrics.jsonl is folded-counters low-growth (see F50). 'Scale poorly' mitigated.

findings.title: Concurrent multi-window sessions cause cross-session selection-token invalidation
findings.expectation_source: Keep active selection tokens valid for their full TTL (30 min) despite minor revision_hash mutations, or scope invalidation to the specific files mutated rather than globally destroying all active tokens.

verify.evidence: selectionTokenCache is keyed on revision_hash and cleared on mutation BY DESIGN: once a window edits a knowledge file the revision changes, so another window's token IS genuinely stale (its plan was over superseded data). Forcing a re-plan_context is the conservative-correct behavior; serving a token over a superseded revision would be the actual bug.

findings.title: Synchronous blocking FS and process execution in multi-store core
findings.expectation_source: Refactor multi-store core to async (fs.promises, execFile-as-promise) to prevent event-loop blockage during cross-store reads/writes or git interactions.

verify.evidence: readKnowledgeAcrossStores/aggregatePendingAcrossStores/listStoreKnowledge have NO callers in server/cli src (grep empty) — the multi-store core is unwired scaffolding (v2.1 global-refactor implementation pending). Not reachable on any live hot path; sync I/O there blocks nothing today. Revisit when multi-store is wired.

findings.title: Ignored/discarded MCP tool arguments (layer_filter & target_paths)
findings.expectation_source: Destructure layer_filter and target_paths in registerPlanContext and layer_filter in registerRecall, pass them to the services, and extend PlanContextInput to declare layer_filter.

verify.evidence: layer_filter declared in recall+planContext schemas (api-contracts.ts:140,419) + default_layer_filter config, but NO service consumed it and tool callbacks dropped it → silently discarded (privacy: layer_filter=team did not exclude personal KP-*). target_paths declared+service-implemented but plan-context tool callback never forwarded it. FIXED: planContext filters rawItems by knowledge_layer (per-call layer_filter ?? config default ?? both); both tool callbacks forward layer_filter; plan-context forwards target_paths. +3 tests; server 736/736; tsc EXIT0.

findings.title: YAML frontmatter injection via unescaped array joining (review merge/patch)
findings.expectation_source: Map each array item through an escaping/quoting helper (quoteIfNeeded / quoteRelevancePath) to escape backslashes, quotes, newlines, brackets before joining.
findings.actual: review.ts flow-array [${arr.join}] 原样拼接, 元素含 ]/换行注入新 frontmatter key

verify.evidence: review.ts flow-array [${arr.join}] 原样拼接, 元素含 ]/换行注入新 frontmatter key

findings.title: Blocking synchronous recursive directory walk on Express event loop
findings.expectation_source: Refactor walkFiles to async fs promises (readdir/stat) or delegate the scan to a worker thread.

verify.evidence: GET /api/scan walkFiles is a blocking sync recursive walk on the Express event loop — real, but in @fenglimg/fabric-server-http-experimental (0.0.0-quarantine, private, EXCLUDED from pnpm workspace). Not in rc2 ship surface; defer with F61/F63/F64 until the HTTP server is un-quarantined.

findings.title: Unhandled git rebase error crashes sync continue/abort flow
findings.expectation_source: Wrap the rebase ops in try-catch in runContinueSync/runAbortSync, record failure/abort cleanup in the state machine/session file, and return a clean error diagnostic instead of crashing.

verify.evidence: run-sync.ts defaultRebaseContinue/Abort (:130,134) ran execFileSync git rebase --continue/--abort with NO try/catch; a failing rebase (unstaged conflicts/no-rebase/dirty) propagated execFileSync's bare throw uncaught → CLI crash, sync-session.json left stale. FIXED: runRebaseStep wraps both, re-surfacing git's diagnostic as actionable GenericIOError (mirrors defaultPull ISS-032). +1 test.

findings.title: Non-atomic session writes and unhandled parse error crash the CLI
findings.expectation_source: Use atomicWriteJson (with fsync) in saveSession, and wrap JSON.parse in loadSession in try-catch to handle corruption gracefully (warn + return null / reclaim new session).

verify.evidence: run-sync.ts loadSession (:76) JSON.parse(readFileSync) with no try-catch → corrupt sync-session.json crashed every later sync with SyntaxError, unrecoverable without manual delete. saveSession (:82) wrote with bare writeFileSync (no tmp+rename) → interruption corrupts. FIXED: loadSession quarantines to .corrupted.{ts} + throws actionable GenericIOError; saveSession writes tmp+renameSync (atomic). +2 tests; cli 884/884.

findings.title: Layer boundary leak / contract inconsistency in plan-context payload trimming
findings.expectation_source: Move payload-budget trimming into the planContext() service layer before the selection token is minted, passing warnings/limits down into the service.

verify.evidence: The selection_token's ai_selectable_stable_ids being a superset of the displayed top_k candidates is benign: a client picking a trimmed-out id still fetches a valid body via get_knowledge_sections (the bodies are in the same layer-filtered corpus — post-F54 the token's set is already layer-scoped, so no cross-layer leak). top_k is a display cap, not an access boundary. The 'trimming in presentation layer' note is a refactor, not a bug.

findings.title: Fragile subcommand short-circuit in fabric config parent command
findings.expectation_source: Check whether the subcommand name exists anywhere in process.argv (process.argv.includes) or query the parsed citty command context instead of hardcoding argv indexes.

verify.evidence: config.ts:274 parent run short-circuit used strict process.argv[3]; `fabric config --target ./p dismiss-slot` made argv[3]='--target' so the short-circuit was bypassed → the interactive panel/uninit-gate launched ON TOP of the already-run subcommand. FIXED: scan process.argv.slice(3) for the subcommand name (order-independent). +1 test.

findings.title: Unhandled promise rejection in experimental HTTP server /events endpoint
findings.expectation_source: Wrap the /events handler in try/catch or an Express async wrapper to send a standard HTTP 500 via sendUnknownError when watcher setup fails.

verify.evidence: events.ts:123 await ensureWatcher (→resolveLedgerPaths throws outside a Fabric dir) in a bare async route (http.ts:319 app.get(/events, handler)); Express 4 doesn't catch async rejection → unhandled rejection / hung connection. FIXED: try/catch around ensureWatcher sends 503 JSON before SSE headers. NOTE: package is workspace-excluded quarantine (0.0.0); verified by standalone typecheck EXIT0 (vitest cannot run — no workspace symlinks).

findings.title: scrubRemoteUrl misses bare userinfo tokens (no ':' separator) -> credential leak
findings.expectation_source: Change the scrubRemoteUrl regex to strip any credential/token in the userinfo segment even without a ':' separator.

verify.evidence: secret-scan.ts:78 scrubRemoteUrl regex required ':' in userinfo, so https://ghp_TOKEN@github.com (bare-token PAT auth, no colon) leaked verbatim into shared registry (fabric-global.json / project fabric.json). FIXED: http(s) now strips ENTIRE userinfo (with/without ':'); ssh git@ convention preserved. +3 regression cases.

findings.title: Experimental HTTP APIs expose unredacted ledger/history with personal-layer leakage
findings.expectation_source: Add a visibility/scope filter to the ledger/history endpoints excluding personal-scope records, and apply redactSecrets before serializing payloads.

verify.evidence: Experimental HTTP /api/ledger,/api/history,/api/replay return raw ledger/state with no KP-* personal-scope filter (+ no PII redaction). DEFERRED: (a) package is 0.0.0-quarantine, private, EXCLUDED from pnpm workspace (not in rc2 ship surface); (b) HTTP server is localhost-bound + bearer-auth single-user dev tool (low cross-store exposure); (c) the redaction half is the F48 PII feature-gap, the KP-* filter half belongs with the global-refactor read-set work when the HTTP server is un-quarantined. Recorded, non-blocking.

findings.title: Unbounded HTTP session growth (no cleanup / idle expiration)
findings.expectation_source: Add session eviction: a background reaper for sessions idle > TTL (e.g. 5 min), or cap max concurrent sessions.

verify.evidence: http.ts sessions Map only pruned on clean onsessionclosed/onclose; half-open/network-drop/orphan-init accumulate unbounded. FIXED: added lastActivityMs + 5-min idle reaper (30-min TTL) closing+evicting stale sessions, cleared in app.dispose, unref'd. Standalone typecheck EXIT0 (quarantine, workspace-excluded).

ship_criteria.name: G-TRIAGE
ship_criteria.desc: critical+high 全部 triage(每簇 verdict!=null)

ship_criteria.name: G-FIX
ship_criteria.desc: confirmed-real 项全部修复且 deterministic 验证

git_commits.subject: F39/F30/F31 meta-counter writers 持 allocator 锁
F62 凭证泄漏 + F61/F64 HTTP experimental
layer_filter 端到端 + target_paths 转发

needs_adjudication.question: KP-* personal/team layer 隔离漏洞(read-set 泄露 + git-committed meta 泄露 + layer_filter 被忽略 + 死守卫)的正确修法 = 已规划 v2.1 layered-registry 重构(required_stores∪personal read-set). 选项: (A) rc2 内做最小补丁(排除 KP-* 出 agents.meta.json + computeRevision, 但会 ripple 到 read path) (B) 整簇 defer 到 v2.1 重构里一并正确解决(推荐, 避免 rc2 引入半成品再被重构推翻). 关系到 commit 点划分.
needs_adjudication.recommendation: B — defer 到 v2.1 重构; rc2 不碰, 防半成品
